text: Standing just outside this glass-walled room, I feel a sense of anticipation and curiosity. The transparency of the barrier between me and the exhibit inside invites reflection on separation—between observer and observed, mind and matter. My own presence is ghostlike, mirrored and refracted in the glass, reminding me how perception is shaped by distance and context. Should I cross the threshold, I sense I will enter a space where meaning is constructed actively, not passively received.
